rationale: Direct alignment. The requirement is fundamentally a consumer-facing transparency and notice obligation for ADMT use before or during interaction.
Strong alignment. Explainability supports the required plain-language description of the ADMT role in an adverse outcome.
Strong alignment. Human review depends on understandable decision factors and reviewer access to relevant decision information.
Direct alignment. Lifecycle documentation is the core ARISE control for maintaining developer records supporting responsible deployment.
Direct alignment. Organizations cannot meet notice, explanation, or human-review duties without an inventory of covered ADMT systems and use cases.
Direct alignment. Regulatory compliance control should track Colorado obligations, owners, evidence, and effective dates.
